business.headline: L&T shares tumble over 7%: Why is the stock down today?L&T shares dropped nearly 7% during the session as investors turned cautious amid escalating tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

Sensex crashes 1,700 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Dalal Street opened sharply lower on Wednesday as escalating geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices rattled global markets. Here are the key reasons behind today's sharp stock market fall.

Panic grips Dalal Street as war tensions sink Sensex by ovr 1,700 pointsDalal Street investors turned cautious as crude oil prices surged and fears grew that a prolonged war in West Asia could disrupt global energy supplies and stoke inflation.

Is the stock market open or closed on Holi, March 4?Wondering if you can trade stocks on Holi this year? While celebrations continue across India on March 4, here's what you need to know about NSE and BSE trading.

India has enough oil stocks, no immediate plan to hike fuel prices: Govt sourcesIndia has 25 days of crude oil and refined oil stocks and is scouting for alternative sources for importing crude oil, LPG and LNG, government sources have confirmed. There is no immediate plan to raise the price of petrol and diesel, according to government sources. However, there is bound to be impact where gas and oil is concerned with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. About 80 per cent of the oil that India depends on comes from Iran and through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz has been shut for the past 48 hours. Energy analyst Chetan Bhutani states that 'strategic reserves can support LPG and LNG demand for about 15 to 20 hot days, if needed, while crude oil reserves are estimated to last about 40 to 45 days in the event of supply disturbances.'

23-year-old turns India's instant house help craze into $100 million startupFrom that single hub in Gurugram to a presence across 10 cities, the growth has been swift. Today, nearly half of Pronto's demand comes from the New Delhi region, with about a fifth from its home base of Bengaluru. The platform now clocks more than 18,000 bookings daily, and over 4,500 professionals worked at least one shift on the app last month.

Iran–US conflict puts India's basmati exports at risk, farmers fear lossesIndia sends nearly 72% of its total basmati exports to Middle Eastern countries. As conflict spreads in the region, uncertainty has started to cloud this important trade. If the war continues for a long time, exports could fall sharply, and farmers may end up paying the price.

India has enough oil stocks for 50 days, looking for more options: Govt sourcesGlobal oil prices rose for the third straight session on Tuesday. Traders are worried that supplies from the Middle East, one of the world's most important oil-producing regions, could be disrupted.

I have Rs 50,000. Should I open a shop or invest in financial assets?Rs 50,000 may not look like a big amount, but it can be the starting point of something meaningful. It could turn into a small business you build with your own hands, or quietly grow through smart investments. The choice you make today could shape your financial future.

Dubai caught in geopolitical storm. Will it hurt its billionaire-friendly image?Dubai has thrived by being the Gulf's safe address and has long promised stability in a turbulent region. Now, with conflict edging closer, that promise is being quietly tested.

India trims gas supply to industries after Qatar halts LNG production: ReportQatar halted its LNG production on Monday as Iran continued strikes in the Gulf in response to Israeli and US attacks. The situation has disrupted energy shipments and pushed up global fuel prices and shipping costs.

Gold jumps nearly Rs 5,000: Will prices continue to rise amid the US-Iran war?The yellow metal is shining once again as geopolitical uncertainty fuels fresh buying interest. As the US-Iran war unsettles global markets, investors are wondering, will gold continue its upward climb from here?

Why is stock market closed today when people are celebrating Holi on March 4?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have declared March 3 as the official trading holiday for Holi 2026 in their annual calendar.

EPFO interest stays at 8.25%: Why has the rate not increased this year?At a time when households are watching every rupee, even a small rise in EPF returns would have felt welcome. Instead, the rate remains at 8.25%. Here's why the retirement body has chosen stability over a hike.

Oil climbs for third day as US-Israel conflict with Iran threatens supplyThe main concern in the market remains the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil and gas supply passes.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

European gas futures jump 42% after Qatar halts LNG amid Middle East conflictEuropean natural gas prices have surged sharply due to the halt in LNG supplies caused by Middle East conflict. This disruption highlights Europe's growing energy vulnerability amid geopolitical tensions.

QatarEnergy to halt LPG production as Middle East tensions deepenThe crisis entered its third day as Iran widened its retaliation following joint US–Israeli strikes that killed its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior military and political figures.

If 50% of insurers offer mental health cover, why is usage below 20%?While it is encouraging that mental health cover is becoming more common, the low usage rate raises questions. Are employees fully aware of what is available to them? Do they know how to access these services? Or are they still hesitant to seek help?
EPFO's interest stays at 8.25% for FY26The last change in the rate came in 2024, when it was raised from 8.15% to 8.25% for 2023-24. Since then, there has been no further increase or reduction.

entertainment.headline: BTS unveils ARIRANG tracklist, signals bold new era ahead of March 20 release BTS has revealed the tracklist for their upcoming album, ARIRANG, set for release on March 20. The album blends Korean cultural roots with global sounds, signalling a new chapter for the group.

Game of Thrones film on Aegon's conquest officially in the works Warner Bros. confirmed a Game of Thrones movie is in development, centring on Aegon I Targaryen's conquest, with Beau Willimon attached as writer. The project is envisioned as a large-scale feature, with HBO also working on a related series.

Rukmini Vasanth, Ashika Ranganath support Sapthami over inappropriate pap angles Kannada actors Rukmini Vasanth, Ashika Ranganath and Divya Spandana have publicly supported Sapthami Gowda following her statement against the use of inappropriate and invasive photography angles by some paparazzi at public events.

Tehran's finale cyber hack: Life imitates art in Iran conflict In Tehran's Season 3 finale, a chaotic hack on traffic lights and communication systems thwarts a nuclear disaster. Now, amid US-Israeli strikes, Iran's networks, apps and signals face similar cyber havoc, turning thriller fiction into unfolding reality.

Selena Gomez kisses Benny Blanco's dirty feet weeks after his viral podcast. Video Selena Gomez kissed her husband Benny Blanco's foot during a recent podcast appearance. This act was seen as a playful response to online criticism he received over a previous viral video showing his dirty bare feet.

Dry day at box office this Holi: Upcoming Dhurandhar 2 vs Toxic clash to blame? Why are there no big Hindi or South releases this Holi? As India celebrates, the film industry seems to be waiting for March 19, when Dhurandhar: The Revenge takes on Toxic. Is it strategic caution, calendar pressure or a sign that the Holi box office window is losing its sheen?

How did Zendaya's mother react to her wedding news with Tom Holland? Read here Zendaya's mother Claire Stoermer has reacted to reports about her daughter already being married to Tom Holland, after stylist Law Roach claimed that the two actors had secretly tied the knot.

Rashmika-Vijay meet fans in Hyderabad after ancestral village visit. Pics Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda attended a post-wedding fan meet in Hyderabad. The couple greeted fans while returning from a visit to Vijay's ancestral village in Telangana.

Mandana Karimi on moving back to Iran amid conflict: India has betrayed me in a way Mandana Karimi said that she has plans to move back to Iran the moment the current regime is gone. She also opened up on losing friends in Mumbai after she protested against the Iranian government.

Report debunks viral claim of Quentin Tarantino's death in Israel-Iran conflict Widespread online rumours alleging filmmaker Quentin Tarantino died in a missile strike amid Israel-Iran conflict have been debunked by sources close to him, confirming he is unharmed.

Sooraj Barjatya says Hum Aapke Hai Koun isn't possible today. Here's what changed Filmmaker Sooraj Barjatya admits a film like his 1994 blockbuster Hum Aapke Hai Koun isn't possible today. His comment came as he reflected on the changing roles of women on and off the screen.

Singer Bad Bunny breaks Sydney attendance record with Australia debut show Puerto Rican artist Bad Bunny made his live debut in Australia, performing two sold-out shows in Sydney and setting a new attendance record at ENGIE Stadium.

Ayesha Khan on memes over shooting Dhurandhar during periods: Everything is joke Ayesha Khan reacted to the online trolling after she confessed about shooting the Shararat song in Dhurandhar while being on periods. She emphasised the need to educate young boys on such issues.

Dhurandhar's Shararat girl Ayesha Khan says her saying she filmed the song while on periods became a national joke: I wasn't embarrassed

Vicky-Katrina to Varun and Kriti Sanon, Bollywood stars share colourful Holi pics Katrina Kaif, Vicky Kaushal, Varun Dhawan and Kriti Sanon offered fans a look into their Holi festivities by posting vibrant photos and messages online.

No one is giving me any National Awards anymore: Five-time winner Shabana Azmi Five-time National Award winner Shabana Azmi said she no longer expects to win India's top honour for cinematic achievement, ahead of her upcoming film, Lahore 1947. She also revealed what truly drives her as an actor now.

Janhvi Kapoor defends brother Arjun against online bullying: No excuse Janhvi Kapoor discussed the persistent social media harassment targeting her and her half-brother, Arjun Kapoor. In a recent interview, she emphasised that there is no justification for bullying.

Sonu Sood reacts after Rajpal Yadav says he doesn't need work Sonu Sood responded after Rajpal Yadav clarified that he does not need to seek work following his interim bail in a cheque bounce case. Sonu said he never implied Rajpal needed help, but urged producers to sign him and pay advances as a mark of dignity.

Kantara actor Sapthami Gowda slams paps who click invasive pics of female actors Kantara actor Sapthami Gowda publicly spoke out against a section of the paparazzi that clicks and circulates inappropriate videos and photos of female actors at public events. She called for restraint and professionalism.

Priyanka Chopra wishes her father had met husband Nick Jonas and daughter Malti Actor Priyanka Chopra wished to see her late father, Ashok Chopra, meet her husband Nick Jonas and daughter Malti. She said her father would have chosen Nick as her life partner.

general news.headline: US-made Kuwaiti jet shot down 3 American F-15s, finds probe: WSJOn the third day of the US-Iran conflict, three US F-15E Strike Eagles went down over Kuwait, which the Trump administration called an apparent 'friendly fire' incident. A preliminary probe now sheds more light on the incident.

Live: Iran launches missiles toward Israel, drone hits CIA station in SaudiUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance continues to intensify on Wednesday, the fifth day of direct military confrontation, with no immediate signs of de-escalation. At least 787 people have been killed in Iran, nearly 60 in Lebanon, around a dozen in Israel, and six US soldiers have also died. Israel launched fresh strikes on Iran and renewed attacks on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including airstrikes in and around Beirut. Iran has retaliated with counterstrikes on Iran and US interests in the Gulf. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the escalating Middle East conflict.

Trump fighting a war he was pushed into, has no endgame: Mehdi HasanAward-winning journalist Mehdi Hasan, in an interview with India Today, said the US-Israel strikes in Iran were an 'unprovoked war of aggression' that was completely unnecessary. He also provided insights into the future of the conflict and why Trump might have joined Israel's war against Iran.

Why Iran attacked US data centres in the Gulf?Amazon Web Services data centres in the UAE and Bahrain were hit in Iranian strikes. This is what Iran's attacks on American data centres in the Middle East tell us about war strategies and tech itself becoming a target.

Starmer is no Winston Churchill: Trump ups criticism of UK PM over Iran strikesThe US President has criticised Starmer several times this week after the UK leader said neither the UK military nor its air bases were involved in the first wave of US and Israeli strikes on Tehran, which killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Russian tycoon who called Epstein's ex-girlfriend Maxwell his soulmate found deadRussian businessman Umar Dzhabrailov, who was recently named in the fresh batch of Epstein files, was found dead in a luxury Moscow apartment. Dzhabrailov once called convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's ex-girlfriend Ghislaine Maxwell his "soulmate."

Yash's Toxic postponed to June, Dhurandhar 2 gets solo run on March 19Yash's much-awaited film, Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grownups, was slated for a grand release on March 19. The film has now been postponed to June, and makers cited Middle East tensions as the reason.

Last year, they beat India in a Test match, which sent the team into a phase of renewed belief and transition. However, back in 2023, this is the same venue where they lost the ODI World Cup semi-final to Australia. Can the Proteas overcome New Zealand, who haven't been at their dominating best in the tournament?

India to buy 5 more Russian S-400s, Op Sindoor hero, amid Middle East conflictAs the Middle East remains on boil following US-Israeli strikes against Iran and subsequent retaliatory attacks by Tehran, India is set to procure five additional squadrons of Russian S-400 air defence missile systems, which played a key role during Operation Sindoor last year.

Will UAE strike Iran after wave of attacks on consulate, port and air base?The UAE has dismissed a media report claiming it is weighing participation alongside the US and Israel in military strikes on Iran. Indian airlines plan to operate 58 flights to Middle East today: GovtThe ministry is continuously coordinating with airlines, airport operators, regulatory authorities and the external affairs ministry to ensure safe operations, orderly restoration of services and facilitation of affected passengers.

RCB to assemble at home ground Chinnaswamy on March 15 ahead of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru will assemble at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on March 15 ahead of IPL 2026. The franchise returns to its home fortress as Bengaluru prepares to host the season opener and key matches.

Cristiano Ronaldo gets a big injury scare ahead of the FIFA World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo suffered a hamstring injury during Al-Nassr's Saudi Pro League win over Al-Fayha. While the Portugal star has begun rehabilitation, the injury has sparked concern ahead of the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

England's spin edge over India: Vaughan picks favourite for IND vs ENG semi-final T20 World Cup: Michael Vaughan believes England's spin trio could outmatch India in the T20 World Cup semi-final in Mumbai. Jos Buttler should be left alone: Harry Brook backs England batter before India semis T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Harry Brook has backed Jos Buttler to bounce back from his lean run of form when England take on India in the semifinal on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium.

The confrontation arose over abuse faced by Candice Warner during Australia's 2018 South Africa tour and led to personal insults and threats.

Lakshya Sen starts All England with statement win over defending champion Shi Yu QiLakshya Sen stunned defending champion Shi Yu Qi 23-21, 19-21, 21-17 in a thrilling Round of 32 clash at the All England Championships, avenging his World Championships defeat with a statement win.

PV Sindhu recalls Dubai ordeal: Situation was frightening, happy to be back homeIndia badminton star PV Sindhu recalled her ordeal while being stuck at the Dubai Airport due to the US bombing of Iran and subsequent closing of the airspace in the Gulf region. Sindhu, who was on her way to take part in the All England Championships, was left stranded in Dubai since Saturday, February 28.

PV Sindhu on Dubai nightmare: Airports shut, heard interception sounds during crisisPV Sindhu opened up about being stranded at Dubai International Airport during the West Asian military conflict. She recalled being stuck at the airport for over 10 hours.

India men's basketball team stranded in Doha amid airspace restrictionsIndia men's basketball team is stranded in Doha due to ongoing airspace restrictions amid geopolitical tensions. Diplomatic and community efforts are underway to ensure the team's safe return to India.

England wary of Abhishek despite poor form: Hope he doesn't score another 100England all-rounder Sam Curran remains cautious of India opener Abhishek Sharma despite his mixed form. The semi-final promises a high-scoring contest with both teams eager to reach the ICC T20 World Cup final.

Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru as Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) are all set to play their five matches of the season at the venue. The historic stadium will also host two play-off fixtures, including the final of the tournament.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.
